Question.title: Question
Question.description: Represents a question submitted by a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Question entity.

nickname.description: The nickname of the user who asked the question.

content.description: The text content of the question.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the question was created.

viewCount.description: The number of times the question has been viewed.

Answer.title: Answer
Answer.description: Represents an answer to a question.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Answer entity.

questionId.description: Reference to the Question this answer belongs to. (Relationship: Question 1:N Answer)

nickname.description: The nickname of the user who provided the answer.

content.description: The text content of the answer.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the answer was created.

AdminConfiguration.title: AdminConfiguration
AdminConfiguration.description: Represents the admin configurable settings for the application's UI/UX.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AdminConfiguration entity.

uiText.description: JSON containing all text elements of the UI.

themeSettings.description: JSON containing configurations for the theme (colors, fonts, etc.).

lastUpdated.description: Timestamp of the last configuration update.

definition.description: Collection of questions submitted by users.  All documents are publicly readable and writable (anonymous submissions).

params.name: questionId
params.description: The unique identifier for each question.

definition.description: Subcollection of answers for each question.  All documents are publicly readable and writable (anonymous submissions).

params.name: questionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent question.

params.name: answerId
params.description: The unique identifier for each answer.

definition.description: Collection to store admin user IDs. Existence of a document for a user ID grants admin privileges.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Auth UID of the admin user.

definition.description: Collection to store admin configuration settings. Only accessible to admin users.

params.name: configId
params.description: The unique identifier for the configuration.

firestore.reasoning:
This Firestore data structure is designed to support Chuchot, an anonymous question and answer platform, focusing on scalability, security, and maintainability. The core principle is Authorization Independence, achieved through denormalization and structural segregation. The structure accommodates anonymous users, real-time feeds, admin configuration, and the 'Best Questions' banner feature. The design emphasizes simplicity for both data access and security rule creation.

**Authorization Independence & QAPs:**

*   **Questions and Answers:**  Authorization is simplified by making all data publicly accessible. Since the platform is designed for anonymous question/answer submissions, there is no need to restrict read or write access based on user identity for these collections.  `list` operations are inherently secure as there are no user-based restrictions. This approach directly addresses QAPs by ensuring that security rules do not act as filters.
*   **Admin Configuration:**  Admin access is controlled via the `roles_admin` collection.  Existence in this collection grants full administrative privileges, which aligns with DBAC principles. Admin configuration data, stored in the `admin_configuration` collection, is accessible only to admins, thus securing the platform's customizable elements.

**Structural Segregation:**

*   `questions` and `answers` are stored in separate top-level collections, segregating data types and simplifying queries and rules.
*   `admin_configuration` is separated from user-generated content, isolating administrative settings.

**Access Modeling:**

*   Public data (questions, answers) is globally accessible.
*   Administrative access is managed using the `roles_admin` collection, employing an existence-based check for authorization.

**Additional Considerations:**

*   Timestamps (`createdAt`, `lastUpdated`) are included for audit trails and potential future features (e.g., sorting, filtering).
*   The structure supports the platform's core features, including real-time feeds, best questions banner (achieved through `viewCount` in `questions`), and admin configuration for UI/UX modifications.
